subject: GRB 191117B: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 15:17:38 UT on 17 Nov 2019, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 191117B (trigger 595696663.360798 / 191117637).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 156.1, Dec = 11.7 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 10h 24m, 11d 41'), with a statistical uncertainty of 7.4 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 54.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn191117637/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn191117637.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn191117637/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn191117637.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn191117637/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn191117637.gif
